title: Cheaper Chinese models are gaining U.S. enterprise usage
friendlyTitle: Businesses are looking at lower-cost AI models from China
summary: CNBC reports that U.S. companies are increasingly trying Chinese-built models as costs for leading proprietary systems rise. It cites OpenRouter data showing the share of tokens used by U.S. companies on Chinese models has exceeded 30% weekly since February and reached 46% at one point.
friendlySummary: Some U.S. companies are trying Chinese AI models because they can cost less while becoming more capable. The report says their use through OpenRouter has grown a lot.
whyItMatters: Model choice is becoming a procurement decision as much as a capability decision. Teams can benefit from testing alternatives on their own workloads, while accounting for data handling, compliance, support, and geopolitical constraints.
friendlyWhyItMatters: People building AI products may have more choices. They should compare quality, price, privacy, and rules before choosing a model.
